prompt: Which of these relations is transitive?
hint: Transitive means: if R(x,y) and R(y,z), then R(x,z). Chain two steps into one.
explanation: A relation R is transitive if whenever x relates to y and y relates to z, x relates to z (∀x∀y∀z((Rxy ∧ Ryz) → Rxz)). 'Is an ancestor of' is transitive: an ancestor of your ancestor is also your ancestor. 'Is the mother of' is NOT — your mother's mother is your grandmother, not your mother. Distance and friendship also fail to chain.
